story.asA: developer implementing the Recipe conversion engine
story.iWant: comprehensive parameter mapping rules between NP3, XMP, and lrtemplate formats
story.soThat: conversions maintain visual similarity ≥95% and handle unmappable parameters gracefully with clear user warnings

subtasks.subtask: Create docs/parameter-mapping.md structure with sections for each format pair
Extract parameter names from XMP and lrtemplate parsers/generators (internal/formats/)
Build comprehensive mapping table with all 50+ parameters
Document field naming conventions and Process Version 2012 specifics
Add examples for each parameter category (basic, color, HSL, advanced features)
Document NP3 Sharpening (0-9) → Sharpness (0-150) formula and rationale
Document NP3 Contrast (-3 to +3) → Contrast (-100 to +100) formula
Document NP3 Brightness (-1 to +1) → Exposure (-5.0 to +5.0) formula
Document NP3 Saturation (-3 to +3) → Saturation (-100 to +100) formula
Document NP3 Hue (-9° to +9°) → Temperature/Tint best-effort approach
Provide pseudo-code and constants for each approximation
Explain visual impact of each approximation
List all XMP/lrtemplate parameters absent in NP3
For each unmappable parameter, decide: warn user, store in Metadata, or discard
Write user-friendly warning messages for each category
Document ConversionError format for unmappable scenarios
Specify behavior differences for CLI/TUI vs Web
Define when to fail conversion vs proceed with warnings

task.title: Document bidirectional conversion logic for all format pairs

subtasks.subtask: Document NP3 → XMP conversion path
Document NP3 → lrtemplate conversion path
Document XMP → NP3 conversion path
Document XMP → lrtemplate conversion path
Document lrtemplate → NP3 conversion path
Document lrtemplate → XMP conversion path
Specify rounding rules for float → int conversions
Document zero-value handling and edge cases
Document metrics for measuring visual similarity
Specify tolerance ranges for round-trip tests
Define critical parameter weights
Create test scenarios for ≥95% similarity validation
Document how to measure similarity with unmappable parameters
Reference existing round-trip tests in lrtemplate_test.go
Specify when to use UniversalRecipe.Metadata map
Define metadata key naming convention
Document serialization format for complex structures
Specify metadata lifecycle
Provide code examples for each unmappable parameter type

task.title: Create implementation guidance and code examples

subtasks.subtask: Specify where to implement mapping logic
Provide pseudo-code for approximation algorithms
Define constants to use
Reference existing implementations
Document common mistakes and gotchas
Provide code snippets showing correct implementation patterns

task.title: Review and validate against existing code

subtasks.subtask: Compare documented mappings with actual implementations in parsers
Compare documented mappings with actual implementations in generators
Verify all parameter names match code
Validate formulas match existing NP3 parser/generator code
Ensure documentation aligns with architecture.md patterns and tech-spec
Cross-reference with UniversalRecipe field definitions

requirements.requirement: Document all 1:1 parameter mappings between XMP and lrtemplate (40+ parameters)
Create mapping table: XMP field name → lrtemplate field name → UniversalRecipe field → range
Include examples for each major parameter category (basic adjustments, color, HSL, advanced)
Verify mappings match actual parameter names used in parsers and generators
Document field naming conventions (e.g., Exposure2012 vs Exposure)
Document approximation formulas for NP3 ↔ XMP/lrtemplate conversions
NP3 Sharpening (0-9) → UniversalRecipe Sharpness (0-150): multiply/divide by 15
NP3 Contrast (-3 to +3) → UniversalRecipe Contrast (-100 to +100): multiply/divide by 33
NP3 Brightness (-1 to +1) → UniversalRecipe Exposure (-5.0 to +5.0): scale appropriately
NP3 Saturation (-3 to +3) → UniversalRecipe Saturation (-100 to +100): multiply/divide by 33
NP3 Hue (-9° to +9°) → UniversalRecipe Temperature/Tint: best-effort approximation
Include rationale for each approximation formula
Create comprehensive list of XMP/lrtemplate parameters that cannot map to NP3
Document parameters: Grain, Dehaze, Texture, Clarity, Vignette, Split Toning, Tone Curves, HSL (8 colors × 3 properties)
For each unmappable parameter, specify whether to: warn user, store in Metadata, or discard
Define warning messages for each unmappable category
Specify behavior when generating NP3 from XMP/lrtemplate with advanced features
Document conversion path logic for all format pairs (6 paths)
Specify when to apply approximation vs direct mapping for each path
Define rounding rules for integer conversions
Specify handling of zero values (omit, include, default)
Document edge case behavior (out-of-range values, missing fields, corrupt data)
Define metrics for measuring visual similarity
Specify acceptable tolerance ranges for round-trip conversions (±1 for integers, ±0.01 for floats)
Document which parameters are critical for visual similarity
Create test scenarios for validating ≥95% similarity goal
Specify how to measure similarity with unmappable parameters present
Document when and how to use UniversalRecipe.Metadata map for preserving unmappable fields
Define key naming conventions for metadata entries
Specify serialization format for complex structures stored in metadata
Document metadata lifecycle
Create examples of metadata usage for each unmappable parameter type
Define ConversionError messages for unmappable parameter scenarios
Specify warning format and content for CLI/TUI vs Web interfaces
Document warning priority levels (critical, advisory, informational)
Create user-friendly explanations for why parameters can't be mapped
Specify when to fail conversion vs proceed with warnings
All mapping rules documented in docs/parameter-mapping.md (new file)
Include code examples showing how to implement each mapping
Provide visual diagrams for complex mappings
Reference architecture.md patterns and tech-spec-epic-1.md requirements
Ensure documentation is clear enough for future format additions
Document where mapping logic should be implemented
Provide pseudo-code for approximation algorithms
Specify which constants to define
Reference existing parser/generator code that implements mappings
Include gotchas and common mistakes to avoid
Document test cases for validating each mapping rule
Specify round-trip test scenarios
Define acceptable failure rates for unmappable parameter warnings
Reference existing round-trip tests in lrtemplate_test.go as examples
Provide guidance on adding new mapping tests

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Defines the requirement for translating parameters between different color models with ≥95% visual similarity. Specifies mapping rules: direct mapping when equivalent, approximation when not 1:1, warnings for unmappable features. No silent data loss allowed.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Documents NP3's 5 core parameters (Sharpening 0-9, Contrast ±3, Brightness ±1, Saturation ±3, Hue ±9°) and their byte offsets in the 1024-byte fixed format. Explains approximation formulas: Sharpening * 15 for Sharpness, (Contrast - 3) * 33 for Contrast range mapping.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Documents XMP's 50+ parameters including Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Saturation, Vibrance, HSL (8 colors × 3 properties), Tone Curves, and Split Toning. Uses Adobe XMP namespaces with XML/RDF structure.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Documents lrtemplate format using Lua table syntax with same 50+ parameters as XMP but different syntax. Nested tables for HSL adjustments. Field order doesn't matter but sorted order preferred for consistency.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Central hub struct containing superset of all format capabilities. 50+ fields with metadata dictionary for unmappable parameters. Serves as intermediate representation for hub-and-spoke conversion pattern.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Complete definition of UniversalRecipe struct with all fields and ranges. Includes Metadata map for preserving unmappable fields. Shows ColorAdjustment and Point structs for complex parameters. Documents parameter mapping strategy table between NP3, XMP, and lrtemplate.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Describes conversion architecture: all formats convert to/from UniversalRecipe hub. Each format package responsible for Parse() and Generate() operations. Eliminates need for N×(N-1) conversion paths.

artifact.reason: Central hub struct defining all 50+ parameters with ranges. Reference for all mapping rules. Includes Metadata map, ColorAdjustment structs, and format-specific preservation fields.
Implements NP3 → UniversalRecipe mappings. Shows exact conversion formulas: sharpening * 10, contrast * 33, etc. Reference for understanding approximation rationale.
Implements UniversalRecipe → NP3 mappings with reverse formulas. Shows clamping strategy and range limitations. Key reference for documenting approximation mappings.
Implements XMP → UniversalRecipe mappings for 50+ parameters. Shows exact parameter names (Exposure2012, Contrast2012, etc.) and XML attribute extraction patterns.
Implements UniversalRecipe → XMP mappings. Shows XML structure generation, namespace handling, and parameter serialization patterns.
Implements lrtemplate → UniversalRecipe mappings using Lua table parsing. Shows regex-based extraction patterns for 50+ parameters including HSL and tone curves.

Implements UniversalRecipe → lrtemplate with complete parameter list (lines 114-250). Shows clamping helpers, zero-value omission strategy, and HSL/tone curve formatting. Primary reference for direct XMP ↔ lrtemplate mappings.
Round-trip test pattern validating parse → generate → parse accuracy. Reference for documenting similarity validation strategy and acceptable tolerance ranges.

go.note: Project uses only Go standard library. No external dependencies for core conversion logic. This keeps documentation focused on parameter mapping logic without framework-specific concerns.

constraint.description: Mapping logic MUST be implemented in generate.go files, not in a separate converter module. Each format generator is responsible for converting from UniversalRecipe to its target format using appropriate approximations and range conversions.

NO SILENT DATA LOSS ALLOWED. When parameters cannot be mapped (e.g., XMP Grain → NP3), they must be either: (1) stored in Metadata map for potential round-trip, (2) trigger user warning, or (3) documented as explicitly discarded with rationale.

Generators MUST omit zero-value parameters to produce clean output files. Zero values indicate "no adjustment" and should not appear in generated files. This pattern established in stories 1-5, 1-6, 1-7.
All parameters MUST be clamped to valid ranges before generation. Use clampInt() and clampFloat() helpers. Out-of-range values should be clamped silently (no error) to nearest valid value.

XMP and lrtemplate use IDENTICAL parameter names (Exposure2012, Contrast2012, etc.) except for format syntax differences. This enables direct 1:1 mapping without transformation. Field names established in stories 1-4 through 1-7.
Conversions MUST achieve ≥95% visual similarity for common adjustments (Exposure, Contrast, Saturation). This is measured via round-trip tests with tolerance: ±1 for integers, ±0.01 for floats.

NP3 format supports ONLY 5 parameters (Sharpening, Contrast, Brightness, Saturation, Hue) in fixed 1024-byte structure. Cannot be extended. Advanced XMP/lrtemplate features (50+ parameters) are unmappable to NP3 and require approximation or metadata storage.
All format packages use ConversionError type with Operation, Format, and Cause fields for consistent error reporting. Pattern established in stories 1-2 through 1-7.

interface.name: Parse([]byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error)
interface.description: Standard parser interface for all format packages. Converts format-specific data to UniversalRecipe hub. Implements format → UniversalRecipe mappings.

interface.name: Generate(*models.UniversalRecipe) ([]byte, error)
interface.description: Standard generator interface for all format packages. Converts UniversalRecipe hub to format-specific data. Implements UniversalRecipe → format mappings with approximations and range conversions.

interface.name: ConversionError
interface.signature: type ConversionError struct { Operation string; Format string; Cause error }
interface.description: Standard error type for all conversion operations. Provides context for failures. Used for validation errors, unsupported features, and generation failures.

interface.name: UniversalRecipe.Metadata
interface.description: Generic map for preserving unmappable format-specific data. Keys use format_fieldname convention (e.g., "np3_unknown_bytes", "xmp_tone_curve"). Enables round-trip preservation of data that doesn't fit universal model.

standards.pattern: Table-driven tests with sample files from testdata/ directories
standards.coverage: Target: ≥90% code coverage per module (established in stories 1-2 through 1-7)
standards.validation: Round-trip tests (parse → generate → parse) validate ≥95% similarity with tolerance: ±1 for integers, ±0.01 for floats
standards.naming: Test functions follow TestFunctionName pattern, table tests use subtests with t.Run()
standards.reference: Story 1-7 achieved 89.3% coverage using this pattern - reference lrtemplate_test.go:772-892 for round-trip test implementation

idea.description: Validate XMP ↔ lrtemplate direct mappings using existing round-trip tests
idea.approach: Extend TestRoundTrip to verify all 50+ parameters map identically between XMP and lrtemplate formats without data loss

idea.description: Test NP3 approximation formulas produce expected ranges
idea.approach: Create test cases for boundary values: NP3 Contrast 0,3,6 → Universal -99,-33,0,+33,+99. Validate reverse mapping produces original NP3 values (with rounding tolerance).

idea.description: Test unmappable parameter warnings are generated correctly
idea.approach: Generate NP3 from UniversalRecipe containing Grain/Dehaze/Texture/Clarity fields. Verify warning messages appear for each unmappable parameter.

idea.description: Test all 6 bidirectional conversion paths maintain data integrity
idea.approach: Create test matrix: NP3→XMP→NP3, NP3→lrtemplate→NP3, XMP→NP3→XMP, XMP→lrtemplate→XMP, lrtemplate→NP3→lrtemplate, lrtemplate→XMP→lrtemplate
Define weighted parameter importance (Exposure: 1.0, Contrast: 1.0, Saturation: 0.8, etc.). Test that similarity calculation correctly weights critical parameters higher.

idea.description: Test Metadata map preserves unmappable data through round-trip conversions
idea.approach: Store ToneCurve in Metadata during XMP→NP3, verify it's retrieved during NP3→XMP. Validate no data loss for round-trip conversions.

idea.description: Document test case examples for each mapping rule type
idea.approach: Create test_examples.md with annotated test cases showing: (1) direct mapping test, (2) approximation test, (3) unmappable parameter test, (4) metadata preservation test
